alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years at Google Devices and Services with two promotions in a global commercial operations and transformation programs role, with demonstrated ability to scale operations and lead cross-functional initiatives
alignments.job_requirement: Build and lead a cross-functional industry team across Value Engineering, Sales, AI Builders and define the processes/structure required

alignments.candidate_evidence: Proven experience in requirements gathering, logic design, UAT, and rollout across transformation programs; background in business operations and product operations indicates stakeholder management capability
alignments.job_requirement: Work side-by-side with customers to align manufacturing solutions against their business strategy and translate complex concepts into actionable insights

alignments.candidate_evidence: Positioning as Strategy and Operations leader with experience scaling global commercial operations and strategic programs; demonstrates strategic thinking across transformation initiatives
alignments.job_requirement: Define the vision for Celonis Manufacturing industry practice and translate this into strategy and prioritized set of actions

alignments.candidate_evidence: Product operations background with ownership of requirements, design, UAT, and rollout; however, experience is transformation and operations-driven rather than native product or manufacturing domain-specific
alignments.job_requirement: Proven record of building software solutions against strategic manufacturing problems and successful use cases solving real-world problems

alignments.candidate_evidence: No explicit evidence of 0-to-1 startup or venture founding experience provided; background is scaled operations at established company (Google)
alignments.job_requirement: High degree of entrepreneurship with experience building in a 0 → 1 environment

alignments.candidate_evidence: No explicit evidence of speaking, presentation, or external-facing leadership provided in candidate profile
alignments.job_requirement: Excellence in public speaking and external presentation and ability to be the face of manufacturing industry internally and externally

gaps.gap: Head of Industry title signals VP/Director-level scope at a mature SaaS company (Celonis is a unicorn with 20+ offices). This exceeds L4-L5 target seniority ceiling. Candidate has eight years at Google with two promotions but no evidence of head-of or director-equivalent leadership of a discrete business unit, practice, or P&L
gaps.mitigation: If Celonis is willing to hire at manager/senior manager scope rather than true head-of/director scope, clarify the role's actual budget, headcount, P&L authority, and external representation requirements. Otherwise this is a stretch beyond target seniority

gaps.gap: No explicit manufacturing or process mining domain expertise. Celonis is enterprise process intelligence; the role owns the manufacturing industry practice and must speak credibly to manufacturing-specific challenges, use cases, and product-fit
gaps.mitigation: Candidate would require steep domain ramp in manufacturing ops, process mining, and Celonis platform. Recommend pairing with manufacturing product lead or customer advisory board during first 6-12 months

gaps.gap: Limited evidence of 0-to-1 or greenfield entrepreneurship. Background is scaling and optimizing existing operations at Google, not launching new products, practices, or ventures from scratch
gaps.mitigation: If the role's primary accountability is scaling an existing practice (not launching entirely new offering), this gap is manageable. Clarify scope with hiring manager

gaps.gap: No explicit external speaking, thought leadership, or public-facing leadership track record. Role requires being 'the face of manufacturing industry internally and externally'
gaps.mitigation: If candidate has presentation or conference experience not detailed in profile, request examples. Otherwise, recommend executive coaching and structured customer advisory board engagement

gaps.gap: Limited consulting or systems integration background. Role may benefit from experience scoping, selling, and deploying enterprise solutions across customer contexts
gaps.mitigation: Product operations and transformation background includes scoping and UAT; may be sufficient if role is product and strategy-driven rather than pure services/consulting

output.summary: Candidate is a strong cultural fit for strategy, operations, and cross-functional program leadership—core competencies required for the role. However, the "Head of Industry" title at a mature SaaS unicorn (Celonis, 20+ offices) signals VP/Director-level scope that exceeds the L4-L5 target seniority ceiling. Candidate has eight years and two promotions at Google but no evidence of leading a discrete business unit, practice, or P&L at that level. Additionally, manufacturing domain expertise and process mining platform fluency are completely absent; ramp would be steep. Strong operations and transformation background may close some gaps, but the seniority, domain, and external leadership requirements make this a significant stretch.
uncertainties: Actual scope of 'Head of Industry' role: is it a true VP/director-equivalent with P&L and budget authority, or a senior manager title with cross-functional leadership scope? This determines seniority fit
Candidate's public speaking, thought leadership, or external presentation experience not detailed; request examples or clarify whether external-facing role is primary accountability
Whether candidate has any manufacturing operations, supply chain, or process mining exposure not captured in profile; domain gap may be addressable with rapid learning
Google scope details: size of team led, budget, P&L, external stakeholder engagement—needed to assess true leadership scope vs. title alone
